title: The Art of Exploitation 🧨
description: Taking advantage of the holes we found.

slides.title: What is Exploitation? 🧨
slides.content:
### Crossing the Threshold

In Chapter 7, we found a vulnerability (an open window). **Exploitation** is the act of actually climbing through that window to gain access to the system.

An **Exploit** is a piece of software or code that takes advantage of a specific bug to give an attacker control.

slides.title: The Goal: RCE 🚀
slides.content:
### Remote Code Execution

The 'Holy Grail' of hackers is **RCE** (Remote Code Execution). This means you can run ANY command you want on the target computer from your own machine across the internet.

quizData.question: What is the difference between a Vulnerability and an Exploit?

options.text: A vulnerability is the weakness; an exploit is the tool used to take advantage of it
A vulnerability is a virus; an exploit is a firewall

quizData.explanation: If a hole is a lock that's broken, the exploit is the specific 'bump key' used to open it.
